event: 72 years old
man
presented with high-grade fever
referred to a nearby clinic
had been diagnosed with influenza B virus infection several days prior
wife and two daughters had been diagnosed with influenza B virus infection several days prior
rapid test using a nasal swab specimen was positive for influenza B antigens
diagnosed with an influenza B virus infection
Oseltamivir was administered
persistent fever and dyspnea at rest
hospitalization
temperature was 38.7°C
blood pressure was 117/80 mmHg
heart rate was 76 beats/min
oxygen saturation level was 88% on room air
fine crackles were audible in lung fields on both sides
white blood cell count 7400/mm3
C-reactive protein 18.1 mg/dL
aspartate transaminase 91 IU/L
L-lactate dehydrogenase 362 IU/L
creatine kinase 793 IU/L
Krebs von den Lungen-6 1772 U/mL
hypoxemia (PaO2=72 Torr)
chest radiography showed ground-glass opacity in lung fields on both sides
chest computed tomography (CT) showed diffuse ground-glass opacity in lung fields on both sides
Gram staining, Ziehl-Neelsen staining, periodic acid-Schiff stain of sputum samples and sputum culture did not show any evidence of bacterial or fungal infection
no evidence of bacterial infection in blood cultures
received high-flow nasal oxygen support
treated with intravenous peramivir (600 IU/day)
treated with antibiotics (initially piperacillin/tazobactam 13.5 g/day and levofloxacin 500 mg/day)
reticular shadow on chest CT worsened
PaO2/FiO2 (ratio of arterial oxygen partial pressure to fractional inspired oxygen) ratio deteriorated to 83
diagnosed with severe ARDS associated with influenza B virus infection
proposed mechanical ventilation and high levels of positive end-expiratory pressure as a treatment of ARDS
family refused to do intubation
Polymyxin B-immobilized fiber column hemoperfusion was performed on day 3
intravenous methylprednisolone 60 mg/day (1 mg/kg) was administered on day 6
patient's respiratory status did not improve
reticular infiltrates on chest CT worsened
noninvasive positive pressure ventilation support
addition of immunosuppressant therapy (intravenous cyclophosphamide 500 mg biweekly)
patient's condition gradually worsened
died on day 129 after admission
autopsy was performed with permission from the family
Pathological findings of lung specimens showed diffuse alveolar damage (DAD) with prominent hyaline membranes
no specific abnormality was observed in other organs, including the heart, liver, and kidneys
